title: erophaca baetica
intro: Erophaca baetica, also known as the Spanish broom, is a flowering shrub native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, kidney problems, and as a diuretic. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and aiding in the treatment of urinary tract infections. The herbal preparations of Erophaca baetica include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, potions, and topical applications.
